blurb:
4Thirteen is a solid sports bar that actually takes the food seriously, which is not always a given. Good spot for watching a game, grabbing drinks with friends, or just a laid-back night out when you don't want anything fancy. The atmosphere is lively without being overwhelming.

The catfish basket gets high marks, and the mac and cheese and candied yams are the kind of sides that make you reconsider every sports bar you've been to before. The hot honey wing basket is another crowd favorite and a good call if you want something with a little kick.

Keep in mind they're closed Tuesdays, and the kitchen doesn't open until 4pm on weekdays. Saturday and Sunday they get going at 1pm, which is perfect for afternoon game days.
address: 755 State Hwy 121 BYP, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 1:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 1:00 PM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Manna is the kind of place you bring someone when you want dinner to be an actual event. Korean BBQ and hot pot at the same spot, with grills right at the table and a staff that genuinely walks you through the whole thing if it's your first time. It's interactive, it's fun, and it's great for a group.

First-timers should know the servers here are a big part of what makes the experience. People rave about how patient and attentive the staff is, explaining exactly how to cook everything so you don't end up with overcooked meat. It takes a little of the guesswork out and makes the whole meal more enjoyable.

With a 4.8 rating across more than 4,000 reviews, this one is hard to argue with. Book ahead if you're coming on a weekend.
address: 2405 S Stemmons Fwy #300, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Delhi6 is the kind of Indian restaurant that works for just about any occasion, whether it's a weeknight dinner, a date night, or getting a group together. With over 4,000 reviews and a 4.6 rating, this place has clearly built a loyal following in Highland Village for good reason. The menu covers traditional curries and dishes from different regions of India, so there's usually something for everyone at the table.

The bar side of things makes it a solid option if you want to linger a bit after dinner. It's a step above your typical strip mall Indian spot in terms of atmosphere.

They're closed Mondays, and lunch service doesn't start until noon on Fridays through Sundays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2420 Justin Rd, Highland Village, TX 75077, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Luci's is one of those neighborhood spots that becomes a regular rotation without you even realizing it. It opens early at 7am every day, which makes it a good call for breakfast, lunch, or a casual dinner without much fuss. The crowd is friendly and the menu hits the comfort food notes most people are looking for.

The sopa is a go-to for a lot of regulars and it's easy to see why. It's the kind of dish that keeps people coming back on a weeknight when you just want something satisfying and familiar.

One heads up: service can slow down when it gets busy, so if you're in a rush, maybe go during off-peak hours or call ahead for takeout.
address: 1305 S State Hwy 121 #166, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you have a sweet tooth and haven't been to Petit Four yet, you're missing out. This little dessert cafe is the kind of place you go when you want something special after dinner or just need a treat on a Friday night. The vibe is cute and Instagram-worthy, but the desserts are the real reason to go.

The strawberry Oreo tart is what gets talked about the most, and for good reason. It's more substantial than it looks because there's a chocolate cake layer underneath. The choco banana pudding tart is another solid pick. Fair warning though: this place leans heavy on chocolate, so if that's your thing, you'll be very happy here.

Just keep in mind they're closed Mondays, and they don't open until 11am on Sundays. Worth planning around.
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 8:30 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:30 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 8:30 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 8:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

restaurant_name: Crowne Plaza Atlanta Perimeter at Ravinia by IHG
blurb:
If you have out-of-town guests coming in and need a solid place to put them up near Perimeter, this is a reliable pick. The Ravinia area is convenient, and the hotel has a restaurant and bar on site so they are not stranded without a car.

The gym and indoor pool are a nice bonus if your guests are the type to actually use them. Complimentary Wi-Fi is included, which matters when people are traveling for work.
address: 4355 Ashford Dunwoody Rd, Atlanta, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
If you have guests coming in for something a little nicer, this is a step up from your standard business hotel. It is on Peachtree Road in Buckhead and the on-site restaurant has a Southern menu worth checking out even if you are not staying there.

The spa and outdoor pool make it a good pick for anyone visiting who wants a comfortable, polished experience without going full resort. It rates well for a reason.
address: 3315 Peachtree Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30326, USA

blurb:
This is a great spot to know about in Sandy Springs when you need a decent lunch and do not want to deal with a full sit-down restaurant. It is counter service, quick, and the bread they use for their sandwiches is the real draw. Bright space, easy to get in and out.

Good for a solo lunch, a casual meetup with a friend, or grabbing a soup and sandwich to go. Just know they close at 6pm on weekdays and 5pm on Saturdays, and they are closed Sundays entirely. Plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Thursday: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Friday: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Saturday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
This place is in a strip mall on Hammond Drive and easy to walk past without a second thought. Do not do that. The food here is genuinely great, and the reviews back it up with a 4.9 rating from over a hundred people. That kind of score is hard to fake.

Perfect for a weeknight takeout run or a quick lunch when you are in the Sandy Springs area. Get there a little before the dinner rush if you can.
address: 230 Hammond Dr #332, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 8:30 PM

blurb:
This is the Thai spot my family comes back to again and again in Sandy Springs. The room is small and cozy, which makes it feel like a real neighborhood restaurant rather than a chain. Good for a weeknight dinner or a low-key date night when you want something reliably good without a lot of fuss.

The curries and stir-fries are what people come for, and they do both well. Portions are solid for the price. They do break between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so do not show up at 3:30pm expecting to get a table.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir #209, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
